source_intake_record.raw_question_text: As shown in the figure, a linear function, $y=-x-2$, intersects with the graph of $y=kx+b$ at point $P\left(2, n\right)$. What then is the solution set of the inequality $kx+b<-x-2$ in terms of $x$?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text: \textbf{Solution:} From the graph, it is known that when $x<2$, the graph of the line $y=kx+b$ is below the graph of the line $y=-x-2$. Therefore, the solution set of the inequality $kx+b<-x-2$ with respect to $x$ is $\boxed{x<2}$.

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says "As shown in the figure"
the needed inequality direction depends on the visual relation of the two lines around the intersection point
multi_step: the task combines identifying the intersection location P(2,n) with determining on which side of x=2 the graph y=kx+b lies below y=-x-2
this is not multiple choice, so the solver must derive the inequality region rather than recognize an option
the answer is a simple one-dimensional inequality, suggesting moderate rather than very high reasoning depth
verifiability: answer_text is a precise short expression: x<2
the target output form is constrained and easy to compare against a normalized inequality

decision_reasons: 题干、答案和图像资产均存在，核心语义未缺失，不应过早 reject。
该题对图像依赖较高，但图像尺寸仅 121x103，视觉关系信息可能不够稳定，降低可用性。
存在 answer_type 标注不一致风险，需要后续复核，因此更适合进入低优先级候选池。
初步打分中的 verifiability_score 较高，说明答案形式明确、后续校验仍具可操作性。
